---
title: "FarmMaps_New"
format: pdf
editor: source
---

```{r}
options(scipen = 999)
library(tidyverse)
library(sf)
library(tools) #for toTitleCase()
library(rnassqs)
wd = "/Users/Hayden/Maps/"

api_key <- "B3E11B15-C923-3D82-9CFC-81AE45FCC6E3"
#https://quickstats.nass.usda.gov/api
nassqs_auth(key = api_key)

county_map_ag = st_read("shapefiles/county_map_ag/county_map_ag.shp")

state_map = st_read("shapefiles/state_map/state_map.shp")
```

```{r}
is_valid_color <- function(color) {
  # Check if it's a valid named color
  if (color %in% colors()) return(TRUE)
  
  # Check if it's a valid hex code (6-digit or 3-digit)
  if (grepl("^#([A-Fa-f0-9]{6}|[A-Fa-f0-9]{3})$", color)) return(TRUE)
  
  return(FALSE)
}
```

```{r}
agcensus_api = function(params){
  #Shoutout u/badassperson for sending me his USDA API-calling code
  
  # count number of records...
  # api won't provide > 50K
  recordcount <- nassqs_record_count(params)$count
  
  years = params$year

  if (recordcount == 0) {
    cat(paste0("No records found for ", tolower(params$commodity_desc),"\n"))
    next
  } else if (recordcount < 49999) {
    df <- nassqs(params)
  } else {
    # glue together multiple years if dataset is too big
    data_list <- lapply(years, function(yr) {
      #we may want to iterate by commodity instead of year if we are requesting a ton of commodities at once
      params$year <- yr
      tryCatch(df <- nassqs(params))
    })
    df <- bind_rows(data_list)
  }
  return(df)
}
```

```{r}
clean_ag_data <- function(df){
  
  df_clean <- df |>
        filter(county_name != "OTHER COUNTIES") |>
        mutate(fips = paste0(state_fips_code, county_code)) |>
        separate(
          short_desc,
          into = c("commodity", "domain"),
          sep = " - ",
          fill = "right"
        ) |>
        group_by(commodity, domain, fips, prodn_practice_desc) |>
        filter(!is.na(Value),
               Value != 0) |>
        arrange(desc(year)) |> #use most recent observation
        slice_head(n = 1) |>
        ungroup()
  
  return(df_clean)
}
```

```{r}
# Parse a single line of user input into a numeric vector (returns NA on invalid)
parse_breaks_input <- function(txt) {
  txt <- trimws(txt)

  if (txt == "") return(numeric(0))           # ENTER -> continue
  if (tolower(txt) == "skip") return("skip")

  # FIX: tell strsplit() to use Perl regex so \s works
  parts <- unlist(strsplit(txt, "[,\\s]+", perl = TRUE))

  nums <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(parts))
  if (any(is.na(nums))) return(NA_real_)
  sort(nums)
}

# Interactive loop: keep calling `map_call()` until user presses ENTER (blank) to accept,
# or enters 'q' to abort. `map_call` is a function that accepts a `breaks` argument.
interactive_tune_breaks <- function(map_call, default_breaks = NULL) {
  # start with default
  current_breaks <- default_breaks

  repeat {
    # call map (map_call should be responsible for making+saving + opening the map)
    map_call(current_breaks)

    # ask user for new breaks
    cat("\nCurrent breaks:", if (is.null(current_breaks) || length(current_breaks)==0) "<auto/continuous>" else paste(current_breaks, collapse = " "), "\n")
    msg <- paste0("Enter new breaks (e.g. 100 200 400 800), press ENTER to accept and continue, or 'q' to quit: ")
    ans <- readline(prompt = msg)
    parsed <- parse_breaks_input(ans)

    if (identical(parsed, "quit")) {
      cat("Quitting by user request.\n")
      stop("Aborted by user")          # or return(NULL) if you prefer to continue
    }

    if (is.numeric(parsed) && length(parsed) == 0) {
      # ENTER pressed => accept current breaks and exit loop
      cat("Accepting current breaks and continuing.\n")
      return()
    }

    if (all(is.na(parsed))) {
      cat("Invalid input. Try again. Example: 100 200 400 800\n")
      next
    }

    # valid numeric vector -> update current_breaks and redraw map in next iteration
    current_breaks <- parsed
    cat("Updated breaks to:", paste(current_breaks, collapse = " "), "\nRe-drawing map...\n")
    # loop repeats and map_call(current_breaks) will be invoked again
  }
}


summary_stats <- function(data){
  print(summary(data$Value))
  print(quantile(data$Value, probs = seq(0,1,0.1), na.rm = TRUE))
}
```


Map-making loop for animal counts
```{r}
#a bit more cumbersome that the version below where you can run each line individually, no national totals yet
animal_map <- function() {
  repeat {
    request <- readline(prompt = "Name an animal or 'done' to stop: ")
    request = tolower(request)
    if (request == "done") break

    params <- list(
      commodity_desc = toupper(request),
      domaincat_desc = "NOT SPECIFIED",
      agg_level_desc = c("COUNTY", "STATE"),
      statisticcat_desc = "INVENTORY",
      year = c(2017, 2022)
    )

    # Load or fetch data
    if (!exists(request)) {
      df <- agcensus_api(params)
      df_clean <- clean_ag_data(df)
      assign(request, df_clean, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    } else {
      df_clean <- get(request)
    }

    animals <- unique(df_clean$commodity)

    for (animal in animals) {

      # ------- Display Name -------
      display_name <- readline(
        prompt = paste0("Enter a plural display name for ", tolower(animal), ": ")
      )
      if (display_name == "") display_name <- tolower(animal)
      if (display_name == "exit") break
      if (display_name == "skip") next

      # ------- Color -------
      repeat {
        color_hex <- readline(prompt = paste0("Enter a color for ", display_name, ": "))
        if (color_hex == "") {
          color_hex <- "maroon"
          break
        } else if (is_valid_color(color_hex)) {
          break
        } else {
          cat("Invalid color. Use a hex code or R color name.\n")
        }
      }

      # ------- Wild / Captive -------
      include_disclaimer <- readline("Is it wild? (yes/no): ")
      caption_text <- ifelse(
        tolower(include_disclaimer) == "yes",
        "Values based on domesticated populations only",
        ""
      )

      # ------------------------------- COUNTY BUBBLE MAP -------------------------------
      repeat {
        max_bubble_size <- readline("Max bubble size? (ENTER for default, 'done' to skip): ")

        if (max_bubble_size == "done") break
        if (max_bubble_size == "")   max_bubble_size <- NA
        else max_bubble_size <- as.numeric(max_bubble_size)

        map(
          data = df_clean |> filter(str_detect(domain, "^INVENTORY"), commodity == animal, agg_level_desc == "COUNTY"),
          map = county_map_ag,
          state_map = state_map,
          title = paste0("Number of ", str_to_title(display_name), " by County"),
          caption_manual = caption_text,
          source = "USDA",
          years = 2022,
          bubbles = TRUE,
          palette = list("high_color" = color_hex),
          max_bubble_size = max_bubble_size
        )
      }

      # ------------------------------- COUNTY CHOROPLETHS W/ BREAK PROMPTS -------------------------------

      make_choro <- function(title, unit_per = NULL) {

        repeat {
          # 1. Entry
          cat("\nEnter manual breaks for:", title, "\n")
          ans <- readline("Breaks (e.g. 10 50 200), ENTER to accept, or 'skip' to skip: ")

          br <- parse_breaks_input(ans)

          # 2. Quit branch
          if (identical(br, "skip")) {
            cat("Skipping map.\n")
            return()
          }

          # 3. Invalid input
          if (any(is.na(br))) {
            cat("Invalid input. Try again.\n")
            next
          }
          
          map_data = df_clean |> 
            filter(str_detect(domain, "^INVENTORY"), commodity == animal, agg_level_desc == "COUNTY")
          
          # 4. Render map
          map(
            data = map_data,
            map = county_map_ag,
            state_map = state_map,
            title = title,
            caption_manual = caption_text,
            source = "USDA",
            years = 2022,
            bubbles = FALSE,
            per = unit_per,
            palette = list("high_color" = color_hex),
            breaks = if (length(br) == 0) compute_breaks(map_data$Value) else br
          )

          # 5. Ask for acceptance
          ok <- readline("Satisfied? (y/n): ")
          if (tolower(ok) == "y" || ok == "") break
        }
      }

      # ------ Per Square Mile -------
      make_choro(
        title = paste0("Number of ", str_to_title(display_name), " Per Square Mile"),
        unit_per = "sqmi"
      )

      # ------ Per Capita -------
      make_choro(
        title = paste0("Number of ", str_to_title(display_name), " Per Capita"),
        unit_per = "pop"
      )


      # ------------------------------- NEW: STATE-LEVEL MAPS -------------------------------

      make_state_map <- function(title, unit_per) {
        repeat {
          cat("\nEnter breaks for:", title, "\n")
          ans <- readline("Breaks (e.g. 1000 5000 20000), ENTER to accept, or 'skip' to skip: ")

          br <- parse_breaks_input(ans)
          if (identical(br, "skip")) return()
          if (any(is.na(br))) { cat("Invalid.\n"); next }

          map_data = df_clean |> 
            filter(domain == "INVENTORY", commodity == animal, agg_level_desc == "STATE")
          #there is also inventory in $ and $/head but I'm not super interested in mapping that
          
          map(
            data = map_data,
            map = state_map,
            state_map = state_map,
            title = title,
            caption_manual = caption_text,
            source = "USDA",
            years = 2022,
            bubbles = FALSE,
            per = unit_per,
            palette = list("high_color" = color_hex),
            breaks = if (length(br) == 0) compute_breaks(map_data$Value) else br
          )

          ok <- readline("Satisfied? (y/n): ")
          if (tolower(ok) == "y" || ok == "") break
        }
      }

      make_state_map(paste0("Number of ", str_to_title(display_name)), unit_per = "")
      
      make_state_map(paste0(str_to_title(display_name), " per Capita"), unit_per = "pop")
      
      make_state_map(paste0(str_to_title(display_name), " per Square Mile"), unit_per = "sqmi")

    } # end animal loop
  } # end main repeat
}
```


```{r}
animal_map()# #954535 chestnut brown
# #C9592E
# view available data at https://quickstats.nass.usda.gov/
#query by group_desc, then iterate through all animals??
```


#this function is too cumbersome, should just get data and make maps manually so that breaks can be customized
```{r}
request = "sheep" #Equine
params <- list(
        commodity_desc = toupper(request),
        #c("SHEEP", "MINK", "HOGS", "GOATS", "ALPACAS", "LLAMAS", "TURKEYS", "BISON", "ELK", "DEER", "OSTRICHES", "EMUS", "DUCKS")
        domaincat_desc = "NOT SPECIFIED",
        statisticcat_desc = "INVENTORY",
        #c("INVENTORY", "OPERATIONS")
        year = c(2017, 2022) #really should be sequencing along by 5 because we don't want non-census years typically because they'd have lower counts
      )

if(!exists(paste0(request, "_national"))){ #don't call API if we already have the data
  for(geo in c("COUNTY", "STATE", "NATIONAL")) {
    params_geo = append(params, list(agg_level_desc = geo))
    df = agcensus_api(params_geo)
    df_geo = clean_ag_data(df)
    assign(paste0(request, "_", tolower(geo)), df_geo, envir = .GlobalEnv)
    assign(paste0(df, "_", tolower(geo), df_geo), envir = .GlobalEnv)
  }
} else {
  tryCatch(df_county = get(request, "_county"), error = cat(paste0("No county data for ", request)))
  tryCatch(df_state = get(request, "_state"), error = cat(paste0("No state data for ", request)))
  
  df_national = get(request, "_national")
}
animal = "SHEEP, INCL LAMBS" #EQUINE, HORSES & PONIES
display_name = "Sheep" #Horses

df_county = df_county |>
  filter(domain == "INVENTORY", commodity == animal)
df_state = df_state |>
  filter(domain == "INVENTORY", commodity == animal)
df_national = df_national |>
  filter(domain == "INVENTORY", commodity == animal)

      #COUNTY MAPS

        map(
          #data = df_county |> filter(str_detect(domain, "^INVENTORY"), commodity == animal),
          data = df_county,
          map = county_map_ag,
          state_map = state_map,
          title = paste0("Number of ", str_to_title(display_name), " by County"),
          subtitle = paste0("US: ", shorten_func(0.1)(df_national |> pull(Value))),
          caption_manual = "Values indicate domesticated populations only",
          source = "USDA",
          years = 2022,
          bubbles = T,
          palette = list(cs_palette = "BurgYl"),#954535
          #max_bubble_size = max_bubble_size
        )

        map(
          #data = df_county |> filter(str_detect(domain, "^INVENTORY"), commodity == animal),
          data = df_county,
          map = county_map_ag,
          state_map = state_map,
          title = paste0(str_to_title(display_name), " Per Square Mile"),
          caption_manual = "Values indicate domesticated populations only",
          source = "USDA",
          per = "sqmi",
          years = 2022,
          bubbles = F,
          palette = list(cs_palette = "BurgYl",
                         reverse_cs_palette = T),#954535
          breaks = c(1,2,5,10)
        )

        map(
          #data = df_county |> filter(str_detect(domain, "^INVENTORY"), commodity == animal),
          data = df_county,
          map = county_map_ag,
          state_map = state_map,
          title = paste0(str_to_title(display_name), " Per Capita"),
          caption_manual = "Values indicate domesticated populations only",
          source = "USDA",
          per = "pop",
          years = 2022,
          bubbles = F,
          palette = list(cs_palette = "BurgYl",
                         reverse_cs_palette = T),#954535
          rounding = 0.01,
          breaks = c(0.05, 0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 1)
        )
        
        #STATE MAPS
        
        map(
          data = df_state,
          map = state_map,
          state_map = state_map,
          title = paste0("Number of ", str_to_title(display_name), " by State"),
          subtitle = paste0("US: ", shorten_func(0.1)(df_national |> pull(Value))),
          caption_manual = "Values indicate domesticated populations only",
          source = "USDA",
          years = 2022,
          bubbles = F,
          #na_to_0 = T, #only if the only NA is DC or otherwise certainly 0
          palette = list(cs_palette = "BurgYl",
                         reverse_cs_palette = T),#954535
          #max_bubble_size = max_bubble_size
        )
        
        map(
          data = df_state,
          map = state_map,
          state_map = state_map,
          title = paste0(str_to_title(display_name), " Per Square Mile"),
          caption_manual = "Values indicate domesticated populations only",
          source = "USDA",
          per = "sqmi",
          years = 2022,
          bubbles = F,
          palette = list(cs_palette = "BurgYl",
                         reverse_cs_palette = T),#954535
          breaks = c(0.5,1,2,3)
        )

        map(
          data = df_state,
          map = state_map,
          state_map = state_map,
          title = paste0(str_to_title(display_name), " Per Capita"),
          caption_manual = "Values indicate domesticated populations only",
          source = "USDA",
          per = "pop",
          years = 2022,
          bubbles = F,
          palette = list(cs_palette = "BurgYl",
                         reverse_cs_palette = T),#954535
          rounding = 0.01,
          breaks = c(0.02, 0.05, 0.1, 0.2)
        )
        
```

#crop_map
```{r}
# crop = "soybeans"
# high_color = "#4cd038" 
# farm_type = "Fields" #Farms or Orchards or Groves
#util_practice_desc == "ALL UTILIZATION PRACTICES", or some handling of this column is necessary for fruits as we only want totals (as opposed to processing, fresh, organic etc.) but for corn there is no total, just grain or sileage
#maybe prompt user with commodity-util_practice pair for display name?

  #unfortunately it seems that tree fruit and berry production is only reported by state
  
  #I need to be careful here because some crops you need to aggregate across varieties while for some there are totals

crop_map = function(crop, color, farm_type){
  
  crop = tolower(crop)
  
params <- list(
  commodity_desc = toupper(crop),
  domaincat_desc = "NOT SPECIFIED",
  agg_level_desc = c("US", "STATE", "COUNTY"),
  statisticcat_desc = c("AREA BEARING", "AREA HARVESTED","PRODUCTION"), #add "ACRES" later and then have the % of land area filter based on area bearing or acres
  year = c(2017,2022)
)

if(!exists(crop)){ #don't call API if we already have the data
df = agcensus_api(params)
				
df_clean = clean_ag_data(df)

assign(crop,df_clean,envir = .GlobalEnv)
}

  land_data = get(crop) |> 
    filter(domain %in% c("ACRES BEARING", "ACRES HARVESTED"), commodity_desc == toupper(crop), agg_level_desc %in% c("COUNTY", "STATE"), prodn_practice_desc == "ALL PRODUCTION PRACTICES") |> 
    group_by(fips, agg_level_desc, commodity) |> 
    summarize(Value = sum(Value)) |>
      ungroup()
  #cherries don't have an all classes and instead have sweet and tart, so I woud have to aggregate across and/or do seperate maps

  production_data = get(crop) |> filter(
    commodity_desc == toupper(crop),
    #util_practice_desc == "ALL UTILIZATION PRACTICES",
    prodn_practice_desc == "ALL PRODUCTION PRACTICES",
    statisticcat_desc == "PRODUCTION"
  ) |>
    mutate(pounds = case_when(unit_desc == "LB" ~ 1,
                              str_detect(unit_desc, "TON") ~ 2000,
                              unit_desc == "CWT" ~ 100, #It's written this way to catch units I haven't implemented yet with the stop()
                              unit_desc == "$" ~ 1,
                              unit_desc == "BU" ~ 1,
                              unit_desc == "GALLONS" ~ 1,
                              T ~ NA),
           Value = Value*pounds,
           unit = case_when(unit_desc %in% c("LB", "TONS", "CWT") ~ "lbs",
                            unit_desc == "GALLONS" ~ "gal",
                            T ~ tolower(unit_desc))) |>
    filter(!unit %in% c("pct by type", "pct by grade")) |> #we don't care about this
    group_by(fips, agg_level_desc, unit, commodity) |> 
    summarize(Value = sum(Value)) |>
    ungroup()
  if(any(is.na(production_data$Value))){
    stop("Units not recognized in production_data")
  }
  
  #iterate through commodity-unit-aggregation level combos
  #prompt user for display name for each commodity
  
  map_types_prod = production_data |>
    select(agg_level_desc, unit, commodity) |>
    unique()
  
  map_types_land = land_data |>
    select(agg_level_desc, commodity) |>
    mutate(unit = "acres") |>
    unique()
  
  map_types = rbind(map_types_land, map_types_prod) |>
    select(-unit) |>
    unique()
  
  cat("Crop types:\n")
  cat(paste0(unique(map_types$commodity)), collapse = "\n")
  
  for(com in unique(map_types$commodity)){
    #enter singular version
    display_name = readline(prompt = paste("Enter display name (singular) for", com, ", 'skip', or 'exit': ")) #assign varieties the same name to merge them
    if(display_name == ""){
      display_name = com
    }
    display_name = tolower(display_name)
    if(display_name == "exit"){
      stop()
    } else if(display_name == "skip"){
      map_types_prod = map_types_prod |>
        filter(commodity != com)
      map_types_land = map_types_land |>
        filter(commodity != com)
      production_data = production_data |>
        filter(commodity != com)
      land_data = land_data |>
        filter(commodity != com)
    }
    map_types_prod = map_types_prod |>
      mutate(commodity = if_else(commodity == com, display_name, commodity))
    production_data = production_data |>
      mutate(commodity = if_else(commodity == com, display_name, commodity))
    map_types_land = map_types_land |>
      mutate(commodity = if_else(commodity == com, display_name, commodity))
    land_data = land_data |>
      mutate(commodity = if_else(commodity == com, display_name, commodity))
  }
  
  map_types_land = unique(map_types_land)
  map_types_prod = unique(map_types_prod)
  
    map_types_land |> pmap(function(agg_level_desc, commodity, unit){
      cat("Processing: ", agg_level_desc, commodity, unit, "\n")
      
      if(agg_level_desc == "COUNTY"){
        county_land_data = land_data |>
          filter(commodity == !!commodity,
                 agg_level_desc == !!agg_level_desc)
      
        if(nrow(county_land_data) != 0) {
          map(
            data = county_land_data,
            map = county_map_ag,
            state_map = state_map,
            title = paste0(toTitleCase(commodity), " ", farm_type, " as a Percent of Land Area"),
            source = "USDA",
            years = 2022,
            bubbles = F,
            per = "acre",
            unit =  "%",
            palette = list("high_color" = color)
          )
          map(
            data = county_land_data,
            map = county_map_ag,
            state_map = state_map,
            title = paste0(toTitleCase(commodity), " Acreage"),
            source = "USDA",
            years = 2022,
            bubbles = T,
            unit =  "acres",
            palette = list("high_color" = color)
          )
      } 
        }else{

      state_land_data = land_data |>
        filter(commodity == !!commodity,
        agg_level_desc == !!agg_level_desc)

      if(nrow(state_land_data) != 0){
  map(data = state_land_data,
      map = state_map,
      state_map = state_map,
      title = paste0(toTitleCase(commodity)," ",farm_type," as a Percent of Land Area"),
      source = "USDA",
      years = 2022,
      bubbles = F,
      per = "acre",
      unit =  "%",
      palette = list("high_color" = color))
    map(data = state_land_data,
      map = state_map,
      state_map = state_map,
      title = paste0(toTitleCase(commodity)," Acreage by State"),
      source = "USDA",
      years = 2022,
      bubbles = F,
      unit =  "acres",
      palette = list("high_color" = color))
      }
        }
      
      #stop to ask for breaks?
    })
  
    map_types_prod |> pmap(function(agg_level_desc, unit, commodity) {
      
    print(paste("Processing:", agg_level_desc, unit, commodity))
      
      if(agg_level_desc == "STATE"){
        level = "State"
        map = state_map
      } else{
        level = "County"
        map = county_map_ag
      }
      
      filtered_data <- production_data %>%
      filter(agg_level_desc == !!agg_level_desc,
             unit == !!unit,
             commodity == !!commodity) |>
        group_by(fips, commodity, unit) |> #merge commodities that were merged by naming them the same thing
        summarize(Value = sum(Value, na.rm = T))
      
      unit_text = case_when(unit == "bu" ~ "Bushels",
                            unit == "lbs" ~ "Pounds",
                            unit == "$" ~ "Dollars",
                            unit == "gal" ~ "Gallons",
                            T ~ unit) #unrecognized unit
      
     map(data = filtered_data,
      map = map,
      state_map = state_map,
      title = paste0(toTitleCase(commodity), " ", if_else(unit == "$", "Sales", "Production")," by ", level),
      source = "USDA",
      years = params$year,
      bubbles = if_else(level == "State", F, T),
      subtitle = paste0(unit_text, " per year"),
      unit = unit,
      palette = list("high_color" = color)) 
     
     #prompt for max_bubble_size?
    
  })

}
```

```{r}
crop_map(crop = "soybeans", color = "#4cd038", farm_type = "Fields")
crop_map(crop = "rice", color = "#4cd038", farm_type = "Farms")
crop_map(crop = "plums", color = "purple", farm_type = "Orchards")
crop_map(crop = "grapes", color = "purple", farm_type = "Groves")
crop_map(crop = "pears", color = "#4cd038", farm_type = "Orchards")
crop_map(crop = "apples", color = "#c22820", farm_type = "Orchards")
crop_map(crop = "cherries", color = "#B62625", farm_type = "Orchards") #split into tart and sweet, but I want total so current code doesn't work
crop_map(crop = "peaches", color = "#f6a192", farm_type = "Orchards")
crop_map(crop = "sorghum", color = "#CC5500", farm_type = "Fields")
crop_map(crop = "strawberries", color = "#fc5a8d", farm_type = "Fields")
crop_map(crop = "blueberries", color = "darkblue", farm_type = "Fields")
crop_map(crop = "oats", color = "#954535", farm_type = "Farms")
crop_map(crop = "potatoes", color = "#954535", farm_type = "Fields")
crop_map(crop = "pumpkins", color = "#CC5500", farm_type = "Patches")
# green #4cd038 brown #954535 burnt orange #CC5500 or #f29d02

unique(grapes$unit_desc)
```


Manual crop map
```{r}
crop = "pumpkins"
color = "#FF7518"
farm_type = "Farms"

crop = tolower(crop)
  
params <- list(
  commodity_desc = toupper(crop),
  domaincat_desc = "NOT SPECIFIED",
  agg_level_desc = c("NATIONAL", "STATE", "COUNTY"),
  statisticcat_desc = c("AREA BEARING", "AREA HARVESTED","PRODUCTION"), #add "ACRES" later and then have the % of land area filter based on area bearing or acres
  year = c(2017,2022)
)

if(!exists(crop)){ #don't call API if we already have the data
df = agcensus_api(params)
				
df_clean = clean_ag_data(df)

assign(crop,df_clean,envir = .GlobalEnv)
}

  land_data = get(crop) |> 
    filter(domain %in% c("ACRES BEARING", "ACRES HARVESTED"), commodity_desc == toupper(crop), agg_level_desc %in% c("COUNTY", "STATE"), prodn_practice_desc == "ALL PRODUCTION PRACTICES", util_practice_desc == "ALL UTILIZATION PRACTICES") |> 
    group_by(fips, agg_level_desc, commodity) |> 
    summarize(Value = sum(Value)) |>
      ungroup()
  #cherries don't have an all classes and instead have sweet and tart, so I woud have to aggregate across and/or do seperate maps

  production_data = get(crop) |> filter(
    commodity_desc == toupper(crop),
    util_practice_desc == "ALL UTILIZATION PRACTICES",
    prodn_practice_desc == "ALL PRODUCTION PRACTICES",
    statisticcat_desc == "PRODUCTION"
  ) |>
    mutate(pounds = case_when(unit_desc == "LB" ~ 1,
                              str_detect(unit_desc, "TON") ~ 2000,
                              unit_desc == "CWT" ~ 100, #It's written this way to catch units I haven't implemented yet with the stop()
                              unit_desc == "$" ~ 1,
                              unit_desc == "BU" ~ 1,
                              unit_desc == "GALLONS" ~ 1,
                              T ~ NA),
           Value = Value*pounds,
           unit = case_when(unit_desc %in% c("LB", "TONS", "CWT") ~ "lbs",
                            unit_desc == "GALLONS" ~ "gal",
                            T ~ tolower(unit_desc))) |>
    filter(!unit %in% c("pct by type", "pct by grade")) |> #we don't care about this
    group_by(fips, agg_level_desc, unit, commodity) |> 
    summarize(Value = sum(Value)) |>
    ungroup()
  
  operations_data = get(crop) |>
    filter(domain %in% "OPERATIONS WITH AREA HARVESTED", agg_level_desc %in% c("COUNTY", "STATE"), prodn_practice_desc == "ALL PRODUCTION PRACTICES", util_practice_desc == "ALL UTILIZATION PRACTICES") |>
    group_by(fips, agg_level_desc, commodity) |> 
    summarize(Value = sum(Value)) |>
      ungroup()
  
  
  if(any(is.na(production_data$Value))){
    stop("Units not recognized in production_data")
  }
  
  #iterate through commodity-unit-aggregation level combos
  #prompt user for display name for each commodity
  
  map_types_prod = production_data |>
    select(agg_level_desc, unit, commodity) |>
    unique()
  
  map_types_land = land_data |>
    select(agg_level_desc, commodity) |>
    mutate(unit = "acres") |>
    unique()
  
  map_types_operations = operations_data |>
    select(agg_level_desc, commodity) |>
    mutate(unit = "operations") |>
    unique()
  
  map_types = rbind(map_types_land, map_types_prod, map_types_operations) |>
    filter(agg_level_desc != "NATIONAL") #use this for subtitles
    # select(-unit) |>
    # unique()
  
  cat("Crop types:\n")
  cat(paste0(unique(map_types$commodity)), collapse = "\n")
  
  for(com in unique(map_types$commodity)){
    #enter singular version
    display_name = readline(prompt = paste("Enter display name for", com, ", 'skip', or 'exit': ")) #assign varieties the same name to merge them
    if(display_name == ""){
      display_name = com
    }
    display_name = tolower(display_name)
    if(display_name == "exit"){
      stop()
    } else if(display_name == "skip"){
      map_types_prod = map_types_prod |>
        filter(commodity != com)
      map_types_land = map_types_land |>
        filter(commodity != com)
      map_types_operations = map_types_operations |>
        filter(commodity != com)
      production_data = production_data |>
        filter(commodity != com)
      land_data = land_data |>
        filter(commodity != com)
      operations_data = operations_data |>
        filter(commodity != com)
    }
    map_types_prod = map_types_prod |>
      mutate(commodity = if_else(commodity == com, display_name, commodity))
    production_data = production_data |>
      mutate(commodity = if_else(commodity == com, display_name, commodity))
    map_types_land = map_types_land |>
      mutate(commodity = if_else(commodity == com, display_name, commodity))
    land_data = land_data |>
      mutate(commodity = if_else(commodity == com, display_name, commodity))
    map_types_operations = map_types_operations |>
      mutate(commodity = if_else(commodity == com, display_name, commodity))
    operations_data = operations_data |>
      mutate(commodity = if_else(commodity == com, display_name, commodity))
  }
  
  map_types_land = unique(map_types_land)
  map_types_prod = unique(map_types_prod)
  map_types_operations = unique(map_types_operations)
  
    map_types_land |> pmap(function(agg_level_desc, commodity, unit){
      cat("Processing: ", agg_level_desc, commodity, unit, "\n")
      
      if(agg_level_desc == "COUNTY"){
        county_land_data = land_data |>
          filter(commodity == !!commodity,
                 agg_level_desc == !!agg_level_desc)
      
        if(nrow(county_land_data) != 0) {
          
          default_breaks <- c(0.01, 0.1, 0.5, 1, 5)
          cat("Percent of Land Area by County\n")
          map_call_prod <- function(breaks_vec){
            map(
              data = county_land_data,
              map = county_map_ag,
              state_map = state_map,
              title = paste0(toTitleCase(commodity), " ", farm_type, " as a Percent of Land Area"),
              source = "USDA",
              years = 2022, #years doesn't do anything anymore
              bubbles = F,
              per = "acre",
              unit =  "%",
              breaks = breaks_vec,
              palette = list("high_color" = color)
            )
          }
          
          interactive_tune_breaks(map_call_prod, default_breaks)
          
          cat("Acreage by County\n")
          map(
            data = county_land_data,
            map = county_map_ag,
            state_map = state_map,
            title = paste0(toTitleCase(commodity), " Acreage"),
            source = "USDA",
            years = 2022,
            bubbles = T,
            unit =  "acres",
            palette = list("high_color" = color)
          )
      } 
        }else{
      
      state_land_data = land_data |>
        filter(commodity == !!commodity,
        agg_level_desc == !!agg_level_desc)
      
      national_total = state_land_data |>
        filter(fips != "98") |> #remove the other states row because it's from the SURVEY source not CENSUS. For pumpkins, there was data for every state but 7.3k acres in "OTHER STATES" presumably these were censored in the vegetable survey but not the census
        summarize(total = sum(Value)) |>
        pull(total) #there is no national row for state acreage so I assume no censoring?

      if(nrow(state_land_data) != 0){
        default_breaks <- c(0.01, 0.1, 0.5, 1, 5) #summary stats aren't very useful for this one because it hasn't been divided by acres yet
    cat("Percent of Land Area by State\n")
    map_call_prod <- function(breaks_vec){
      map(data = state_land_data,
        map = state_map,
        state_map = state_map,
        title = paste0(toTitleCase(commodity)," ",farm_type," as a Percent of Land Area"),
        source = "USDA",
        years = 2022,
        bubbles = F,
        per = "acre",
        unit =  "%",
        breaks = breaks_vec,
        palette = list("high_color" = color)
        )
    }
    
    interactive_tune_breaks(map_call_prod, default_breaks)
    
    cat("Acreage by State\n")
    
    summary_stats(state_land_data)
    default_breaks <- compute_breaks(state_land_data$Value)
    
    map_call_prod <- function(breaks_vec){
      map(data = state_land_data,
        map = state_map,
        state_map = state_map,
        title = paste0(toTitleCase(commodity)," Acreage"),
        subtitle = paste0("US Total: ", append_unit("", " acres")(national_total)),
        source = "USDA",
        years = 2022,
        bubbles = F,
        unit =  "acres",
        breaks = breaks_vec,
        # caption_manual = if_else(!is.na(other_states), paste0("Other States: ", shorten(other_states), " acres"), ""),
        palette = list("high_color" = color)
        )
    }
    interactive_tune_breaks(map_call_prod, default_breaks)
        }
      }
    })
  
    map_types_prod |> pmap(function(agg_level_desc, unit, commodity) {
      
    print(paste("Processing:", agg_level_desc, unit, commodity))
      
      if(agg_level_desc == "STATE"){
        level = "State"
        map = state_map
      } else{
        level = "County"
        map = county_map_ag
      }
      
      filtered_data <- production_data %>%
      filter(agg_level_desc == !!agg_level_desc,
             unit == !!unit,
             commodity == !!commodity) |>
        group_by(fips, commodity, unit) |> #merge commodities that were merged by naming them the same thing
        summarize(Value = sum(Value, na.rm = T))
      
      unit_text = case_when(unit == "bu" ~ "Bushels",
                            unit == "lbs" ~ "Pounds",
                            unit == "$" ~ "Dollars",
                            unit == "gal" ~ "Gallons",
                            T ~ unit) #unrecognized unit

    dollar = if_else(unit == "$", "$", "")
    
    if(level == "State"){
      
    other_states = filtered_data |>
      filter(fips == "98",
             unit == unit) |>
      pull(Value)
      
    cat(paste0(if_else(unit == "$", "Sales", "Production"), " by State\n"))
    summary_stats(filtered_data)
    default_breaks = compute_breaks(filtered_data$Value)
    
      map_call_prod <- function(breaks_vec){
       map(data = filtered_data,
        map = map,
        state_map = state_map,
        title = paste0(toTitleCase(commodity), " ", if_else(unit == "$", "Sales", "Production")),
        source = "USDA",
        years = params$year,
        bubbles = F,
        subtitle = paste0(unit_text, " per year"),
        unit = unit,
        breaks = breaks_vec,
        caption_manual = if_else(!is.na(other_states), paste0("Other States: ", append_unit(dollar, if_else(unit == "$", "", paste0(" ", unit)))(other_states)), ""),
        palette = list("high_color" = color)
        )
    }
    interactive_tune_breaks(map_call_prod, default_breaks)
      
    } else {

    cat(paste0(if_else(unit == "$", "Sales", "Production"), " by County\n"))
     map(data = filtered_data,
      map = map,
      state_map = state_map,
      title = paste0(toTitleCase(commodity), " ", if_else(unit == "$", "Sales", "Production")),
      source = "USDA",
      years = params$year,
      bubbles = T,
      subtitle = paste0(unit_text, " per year"),
      unit = unit,
      palette = list("high_color" = color)
      )
    }
    
  })
    
    
  map_types_operations |> pmap(function(agg_level_desc, unit, commodity) {
    #I don't do anything to handle fips == 98 for this, don't think it will come up
      
    print(paste("Processing:", agg_level_desc, unit, commodity))
      
      if(agg_level_desc == "COUNTY"){
        county_operations_data = operations_data |>
          filter(commodity == !!commodity,
                 agg_level_desc == !!agg_level_desc)
      
        if(nrow(county_operations_data) != 0) {
          cat("Operations by County\n")
          
          map(
            data = county_operations_data,
            map = county_map_ag,
            state_map = state_map,
            title = paste0(toTitleCase(commodity), " ", farm_type),
            source = "USDA",
            years = 2022,
            palette = list("high_color" = color)
          )
      } 
        }else{

      state_operations_data = operations_data |>
        filter(commodity == !!commodity,
        agg_level_desc == !!agg_level_desc)

      if(nrow(state_operations_data) != 0){
        
        cat("Operations by State\n")
        summary_stats(state_operations_data)
        default_breaks = compute_breaks(state_operations_data$Value)
    
      map_call_prod <- function(breaks_vec){
        
      map(data = state_operations_data,
          map = state_map,
          state_map = state_map,
          title = paste0(toTitleCase(commodity)," ",farm_type),
          subtitle = paste0("US Total: ", shorten(sum(state_operations_data$Value))),
          source = "USDA",
          years = 2022,
          bubbles = F,
          breaks = breaks_vec,
          palette = list("high_color" = color)
          )
        }
      interactive_tune_breaks(map_call_prod, default_breaks)
      }
    }
    
  })

```

```{r}
#maple syrup taps map
crop = "maple syrup"
    map(data = get(crop) |> filter(domain == "NUMBER OF TAPS", commodity_desc == toupper(crop), class_desc == "ALL CLASSES"),
      map = county_map_ag,
      state_map = state_map,
      title = "Maple Syrup Taps",
      source = "USDA",
      years = 2022,
      bubbles = T,
      unit = "taps",
      max_bubble_size = 10,
      palette = list("high_color" = "#A02900",
                     "low_color" = "beige")
      )
```

#CA-specific map?
things that are grown almost exclusively in CA:
    #walnuts
    #tomatoes
    #almonds
    #peaches
    #grapes
    #broccoli
